title: quercus treubiana
intro: Quercus treubiana is a species of oak tree native to Indonesia, belonging to the Fagaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with its leaves, bark, and roots exhibiting anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Quercus treubiana include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and provide antioxidant properties, which may help protect against oxidative stress and cell damage. Herbal preparations of Quercus treubiana are often made into teas, infusions, and extracts, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
